title: Epsilon Characterization of Infimum
statement: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ and let $t\in\mathbb{R}$. The element $t$ satisfies the \emph{epsilon characterization of the infimum} of $A$ if and only if $t$ is a lower bound of $A$ and for every $\varepsilon>0$ there exists $a\in A$ such that $a<t+\varepsilon$.
